headline: Sandisk Spun Off From Western Digital Last Year. Now It’s a $200 Billion Company.
summary: Sandisk  shares rose once more Tuesday, with the memory-chip company joining the likes of  McDonald’s Verizon  and  PepsiCo  when it comes to market value.  Sandisk  stock at Tuesday’s closing bell surpassed a $200 billion market capitalization for the first time since the company completed its spinoff from  Western Digital  on Feb. 24, 2025, according to Dow Jones Market Data.  Sandisk advanced 12% to $1,406.32 on Tuesday, as investors continued betting that the boom in construction of artificial-intelligence data centers will drive up memory demand.

headline: PepsiCo’s Health Push And Human-Rights Scrutiny Might Change The Case For Investing In PEP
summary: In late April and early May 2026, PepsiCo brands announced a string of initiatives, including Gatorade’s "Body of Science" women’s hydration research program, new functional and protein-enhanced snacks and beverages, and Tostitos’ entry into refrigerated guacamole, while a shareholder proposal called for stronger human-rights reporting across PepsiCo’s value chain. These developments highlight how PepsiCo is simultaneously pushing into health-focused product innovation and facing rising...

headline: Coca-Cola Trades at Valuation Premium: Overvalued or Apt for Entry?
summary: KO's premium valuation raises entry concerns, even as volume growth, margins and brand strength support its stock momentum.

headline: PepsiCo vs Coca-Cola: A Decade-Long Fight for Dominance
summary: PepsiCo (NASDAQ: PEP) and Coca-Cola (NYSE: KO) just delivered first quarter results that expose a widening gap between the two beverage giants. Coke leaned on Zero Sugar momentum and a near pure-play concentrate model. Pepsi leaned on snacks, international resilience, and an affordability reset to nudge North American volumes back to life. headline: PepsiCo and TalusAg Announce Low-carbon Ammonia Attribute Agreement to Help Accelerate Fertilizer Decarbonization
summary: PepsiCo (NASDAQ: PEP) today announced a new collaboration with agriculture technology company, TalusAg, that aims to advance fertilizer decarbonization across global agricultural supply chains through low-carbon ammonia environmental attributes, marking PepsiCo's first executed transactions of this kind.

headline: 3 Unpopular Stocks We Keep Off Our Radar
summary: Wall Street has issued downbeat forecasts for the stocks in this article. These predictions are rare - financial institutions typically hesitate to say bad things about a company because it can jeopardize their other revenue-generating business lines like M&A advisory.
